createSurvey(string,string).details: Create a new survey with a globally unique ID (generated client-side)

params.ipfsCid: IPFS content identifier for survey metadata

isNullifierUsed(string,address).details: Check if a specific nullifier has already been used

registerBatch(string,address).details: Register a new card batch for a survey.      The batchId is the address of an ephemeral wallet derived off-chain      from a single owner signature. This allows all cards in the batch to      be signed locally without per-card wallet popups, while keeping      on-chain verification trustless.      Batches are immutable once registered — this protects already-printed      cards from being invalidated by the survey owner.

params.batchId: Ephemeral batch wallet address (also serves as the batch identifier)

updateSurvey(string,string).details: Update survey IPFS CID — survey content is mutable post-creation

validateCard(string,bytes,address,string.details: Validate a card and record the submission.      Verifies that the nullifier was signed by the registered batch wallet,      ensuring only legitimately printed cards can be submitted.      Each card can only be submitted once.

params.batchId: Batch wallet address this card belongs to
params.nullifier: Unique card identifier from QR code
params.signature: Signature produced by the ephemeral batch wallet

userdoc.notice: Stores surveys and validates anonymous survey card submissions. Card generation flow (off-chain):   1. Survey owner signs a random seed once → derives an ephemeral batch wallet (one popup)   2. Batch wallet address is registered on-chain as the batchId via registerBatch()   3. Each card's nullifier is signed locally by the batch wallet (no further popups)   4. Cards are printed as QR codes containing: nullifier, batchId, signature, surveyId Card submission flow (on-chain):   1. validateCard() recovers the signer from the card's signature   2. Verifies signer === registered batch wallet (batchId)   3. Marks nullifier as used to prevent double submission Key design decisions:   - batchId === batch wallet address — no separate UUID needed   - Batch signers are immutable once registered — protects printed cards   - Batches are scoped to a survey — a batch wallet cannot be reused across surveys
